summary: Iteration 6 follow-up pass on the two remaining bugs from iteration_5. Backend: 3/3 new iteration_6 tests pass + regression 7/7 iteration_5 tests still pass (10/10 total). The move_student_batch endpoint no longer crashes when moving a student to Self-paced (batch_id=null) — the code correctly initialises target_batch_name='Self-paced' before the conditional and overwrites when a batch is provided. Frontend: EnrollModal now renders BOTH data-testid='tab-razorpay' AND data-testid='tab-upi' unconditionally on paid courses (visible in preview where Razorpay env keys are empty). Clicking the Online Payment tab reveals data-testid='razorpay-not-configured' with the copy 'Online payments not configured yet. Please pay via UPI (tab beside).' — confirmed via screenshot after native element.click().

issues: Purely cosmetic: after switching to the Razorpay tab when disabled, the Pay ₹500 button is not rendered so the modal shrinks. Layout is fine but a hint like 'Contact admin at ...' would improve UX for stuck users.

critical_code_review_comments: courses.py move_student_batch — the fix pattern (initialise target_batch_name to 'Self-paced' before the conditional) is correct and idiomatic. Consider extracting a small helper if this pattern repeats elsewhere.

EnrollModal.jsx — the default tab state is still hard-coded to 'razorpay' on line 17; the effect switches to 'upi' when razorpay_enabled=false. This creates a one-frame flicker where the Razorpay banner briefly renders before the effect runs. Consider computing the initial tab from settings synchronously OR using useReducer with a lazy initializer. Non-blocking.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — both tabs render, banner content verified via screenshot after tab-razorpay click

test_credentials: Admin: admin@rgpacademy.com / Admin@123 · Teacher: teacher@rgpacademy.com / Teacher@123 · Student: student@rgpacademy.com / Student@123. Iteration 6 also creates fresh TEST_iter6_* students for hermetic tests.
seed_data_creation: 1-2 additional TEST_iter6_* student accounts + enrolments in the first teacher course with a batch (non-blocking, prefixed for later cleanup).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both iteration_5 flagged bugs are fixed and verified end-to-end. Backend endpoint /api/courses/{cid}/students/{sid}/batch handles batch_id=null cleanly. EnrollModal renders both tabs unconditionally (razorpay tab always visible; banner appears when razorpay_enabled=false). Note: Playwright's force=True click occasionally fails to trigger React's onClick on the Razorpay tab in the modal — a real user click and JS element.click() both work correctly; this is a Playwright quirk, not a product bug. Downstream tests should use page.evaluate to synthesise clicks or drop force=True.
rca of the issue: iter5 bug (a): The code read `batch['name']` inside an f-string outside the `if body.batch_id:` guard, causing UnboundLocalError whenever a teacher moved a student to Self-paced. Fix: initialise `target_batch_name = 'Self-paced'` before the conditional and overwrite inside the guard. Reproduction: `curl -X PUT .../courses/{cid}/students/{sid}/batch -d '{"batch_id":null}'` → previously 500, now 200. iter5 bug (b): EnrollModal wrapped the Razorpay tab button in `{settings?.razorpay_enabled && ...}` — when env keys were empty (preview), the button did not render and the review spec was unverifiable. Fix: remove the wrapper so the tab button always renders; only the inner Pay button / banner is gated on `settings?.razorpay_enabled`. Verified visually.
